title: Canvas API Platform
description: Headless CMS and content API with GraphQL, webhooks, and multi-tenant architecture.
overview: Canvas is a headless content platform I built to power marketing sites for agency clients. It provides a GraphQL API, webhook delivery, media transforms, and tenant isolation — all self-hostable.
challenge: Agencies needed a CMS cheaper than Contentful with better DX than Strapi. Multi-tenancy, rate limiting, and webhook reliability were non-negotiable for production client sites.
solution: I designed a schema-per-tenant PostgreSQL model with row-level security, a GraphQL layer via Apollo Server, and S3-compatible media storage. Webhooks use a retry queue with exponential backoff and dead-letter logging.
results: Powers 14 client sites with 99.97% uptime over 12 months. API p95 response time under 45ms. Reduced per-client CMS costs by 70% vs. previous SaaS solution.
